headline: LTAT fund's RM4.1 billion investment losses under criminal review
context: The Armed Forces Fund Board's RM4.1 billion in investment losses between 2018-2023 are under criminal investigation by MACC following the Auditor General's special audit report.

cards.big: RM4.1 billion in military pension fund losses now under criminal investigation
cards.sub: Armed forces members' retirement savings depleted by questionable investments

cards.big: LTAT Act 1973 gives board wide investment discretion with limited oversight
cards.sub: Investment committee operated without independent risk management review for 5 years

cards.big: Losses included RM1.8 billion in Boustead Holdings property ventures
cards.sub: Fund concentrated 44% of portfolio in a single underperforming conglomerate

cards.big: LTAT board had 3 serving generals with no financial industry experience
cards.sub: Military expertise does not translate to investment management competency

cards.big: Soldiers trusted the government with their retirement — the government trusted unqualified boards with their money
Should military pension funds be managed by professional fund managers with fiduciary obligations?
